improved_prompt: You are a chess player analyzing a chess variant. In this variant, the knights are starting on squares typically occupied by bishops (c1, f1 for White and c8, f8 for Black), and bishops are starting where knights normally sit (b1, g1 for White and b8, g8 for Black). Please keep this in mind as you analyze the moves. Remember that knights maintain their movement pattern, moving in an 'L' shape (two squares in one direction and one square perpendicular), while bishops only move diagonally from their new starting points. Analyze the given opening moves and determine if they are legal under these new placements. Answer 'legal' if all moves conform to the rules of this variant, or 'illegal' if any move breaks the rules. Provide a brief justification for the legality of the moves and enclose your final answer in <ans> and </ans> format.
improvement_summary: The improved prompt clarifies the starting positions of pieces and their movement, enhancing the accuracy of legality evaluations.
